skill.name: capa-officer
skill.description: Guides Corrective and Preventive Action work for quality management teams, including investigations, root cause analysis, action planning, and effectiveness checks.
skill.summary: Manage CAPA investigations, action plans, effectiveness checks, and quality system improvement.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, shell backtick execution, environment access, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives caused by CAPA terminology, Markdown code fences, inline file names, placeholder reference text, and normal quality-system wording. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or executable command invocation was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations contain CAPA description text, placeholder asset examples, and reference document headings. No cryptographic API, hashing function, encryption routine, or password-handling logic is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are descriptive Markdown or placeholder text, not executable code. The duplicate SKILL.md line 3 hit appears to come from ordinary CAPA wording rather than a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misread As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged SKILL.md locations are Markdown fenced blocks and inline code formatting for resource names. They do not instruct the model to execute shell commands and do not contain Ruby backtick execution syntax in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a CAPA workflow diagram and resource list. No command strings, shell metacharacters, or user-controlled execution path are present.

low_findings.title: Environment And Network Pattern Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 124 discusses CAPA source categorization, not environment variables. SKILL.md line 159 discusses training requirements and contains no network probing, host discovery, or remote connection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are quality-management prose. There is no evidence of process environment access, sockets, scanning commands, or reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Quality teams need consistent CAPA records that connect problems, causes, actions, and verification evidence. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure investigations, action plans, trends, and closure reviews.
actual_capabilities: Drafts CAPA problem statements, scope, impact summaries, and investigation plans.
Selects suitable root cause analysis methods such as 5 Why, fishbone, FMEA, or fault tree analysis.
Builds corrective and preventive action plans with owners, timelines, deliverables, and success criteria.
Defines effectiveness verification methods and closure criteria for completed CAPAs.
Organizes CAPA metrics, trend categories, and management review talking points.
limitations: Does not replace review by a qualified quality, regulatory, or legal professional.
Does not validate compliance against the latest regulations without current source material.
Does not execute CAPA tracking software or update quality system records by itself.
Uses placeholder reference and asset files, so teams must provide their own procedures and templates.

use_cases.title: Investigate a Quality Event
use_cases.description: Create a structured investigation plan with data needs, scope, impact assessment, and root cause method selection.

use_cases.title: Plan Corrective Actions
use_cases.description: Turn confirmed root causes into action plans with owners, dates, evidence needs, and measurable success criteria.

use_cases.title: Prepare Management Review
use_cases.description: Summarize CAPA status, overdue items, recurrence patterns, and effectiveness trends for leadership review.

prompt_templates.title: Start a CAPA
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new CAPA record.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me draft a CAPA initiation record for this issue: [describe event]. Include problem statement, scope, impact, immediate containment, and missing information.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an RCA Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CAPA issue and recommend the best root cause analysis method. Explain why, list required evidence, and outline the investigation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Action Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request after root cause confirmation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using these confirmed root causes, create a corrective and preventive action plan with owners, timelines, deliverables, verification evidence, and escalation triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Assess CAPA Effectiveness
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether this CAPA is ready for closure. Review the actions, verification data, recurrence evidence, residual risk, and any gaps that need follow-up.

output_examples.input: A packaging label mix-up was found during final inspection.
output_examples.output: A CAPA initiation summary with containment steps, affected-lot assessment, likely investigation paths, required records, and a recommended root cause method.

output_examples.input: The team identified training gaps and unclear work instructions as root causes.
output_examples.output: A corrective and preventive action plan with procedure updates, competency checks, owners, due dates, effectiveness metrics, and closure evidence.

output_examples.input: Management needs a quarterly CAPA trend update.
output_examples.output: A concise review covering open CAPAs, overdue items, recurrence rates, common root cause themes, and recommended preventive actions.

best_practices: Provide the event description, affected products or processes, known dates, and available evidence.
Separate immediate containment, root cause correction, and preventive action in every request.
Ask for measurable effectiveness criteria before closing a CAPA.
anti_patterns: Do not use generic root causes such as human error without system-level analysis.
Do not close a CAPA based only on action completion without effectiveness evidence.
Do not use the skill as the only source for regulatory interpretation.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure CAPA investigations, root cause analysis, action plans, metrics, and effectiveness verification.

faq.question: Can it make final compliance decisions?
faq.answer: No. A qualified quality or regulatory professional should review final decisions and records.

faq.question: Which RCA methods does it support?
faq.answer: It references 5 Why, fishbone, fault tree analysis, human factors analysis, and FMEA.

faq.question: Can it create CAPA reports for leadership?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize status, trend, recurrence, overdue, and effectiveness information for management review.

faq.question: Does it include ready-to-use templates?
faq.answer: The package only contains placeholder asset and reference files, so teams should supply approved templates.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated industries?
faq.answer: It can support regulated quality workflows, but teams must verify outputs against current procedures and regulations.
